mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.13% as of 2019-08-15 reflects recent Fed rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, which sustains corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151,541 thousand as of 2019-08-15 reflects continued employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month growth rate and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive PAYEMS growth does not correlate with positive forward returns in > 50% of test periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and foreshadows earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: demand slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines > 1.5% MoM, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. Accelerating INDPRO signals robust supply-side fundamentals supporting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When INDPRO 3-month growth > 0.5%, 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both series show month-over-month growth, equity returns accelerate over the next 5 trading days due to forward-looking earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase > 0.5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,191 thousand and RSAFS at 526,057 million as of 2019-07-15
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS grow > 0.5% MoM, 5-day forward returns are negative in > 52% of triggered instances, or interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower Rank IC than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that foreshadows earnings pressure and equity selloffs over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% and PAYEMS at 151,541 thousand as of 2019-08-15
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: When both UNRATE rises > 0.3pp and PAYEMS declines, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or joint interaction signal produces lower predictive power than either series independently.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary tightening that constrains equity valuations, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 256.3 and FEDFUNDS at 2.13% as of 2019-08-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: When both inflation > 2.5% YoY and FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction effect does not materially improve predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized signals late-cycle weakness and earnings compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon in late-cycle expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,023.022 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2019-04-15
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized, 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.
